In @.env.example around lines 8 - 20, Add GLACIER_IMAGE_FILESYSTEM_ROOT and GLACIER_S3_SERVER_SIDE_ENCRYPTION to the environment-variable template near the related filesystem and S3 settings, using appropriate example/default values consistent with application.properties. suggestions:
# Immutable after the first image upload: FILESYSTEM, POSTGRESQL, or S3.
GLACIER_IMAGE_BACKEND=FILESYSTEM
GLACIER_S3_ENDPOINT=
GLACIER_S3_REGION=us-east-1
GLACIER_S3_BUCKET=glacier-notes
GLACIER_S3_PATH_STYLE=true
GLACIER_S3_ACCESS_KEY_FILE=
GLACIER_S3_SECRET_KEY_FILE=
GLACIER_S3_SERVER_SIDE_ENCRYPTION=
GLACIER_IMAGE_FILESYSTEM_ROOT=

# Local-only ports used by compose.minio.yaml.
GLACIER_MINIO_API_PORT=9002
GLACIER_MINIO_CONSOLE_PORT=9003

In @backend/src/main/resources/db/migration/V6__image_assets.sql around lines 18 - 20, Extend the migration’s instance_settings schema and corresponding GlacierConfiguration persistence to add admin-editable storage for allowedImageTypes, maximumImageBytes, and perUserStorageQuotaBytes alongside image_orphan_grace_hours. Ensure AdminSettings and AdminSettingsUpdate PATCH handling reads and writes these three fields through the same persisted settings path.
suggestions:
ALTER TABLE instance_settings
    ADD COLUMN image_orphan_grace_hours INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 24
        CHECK (image_orphan_grace_hours BETWEEN 1 AND 720),
    ADD COLUMN allowed_image_types VARCHAR(32)[] NOT NULL DEFAULT ARRAY['image/png','image/jpeg','image/webp'],
    ADD COLUMN maximum_image_bytes BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 41943040
        CHECK (maximum_image_bytes BETWEEN 1048576 AND 52428800),
    ADD COLUMN per_user_storage_quota_bytes BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 10485760000
        CHECK (per_user_storage_quota_bytes BETWEEN 10485760 AND 1099511627776);
